title: inspect --redact + redaction map proof
manager: Provably-safe redaction — `inspect --redact` uses a verifier-emitted map to pseudonymize data that no guard reads, so a redacted tape stays bit-identical on replay while sensitive values are removed. This is what lets you share a run for debugging or compliance without leaking secrets: the verifier proves which data is load-bearing (and flags it) versus safely removable, so redaction can never accidentally change the machine's behavior.
cs: `inspect --redact` via a verifier-emitted map (data unread-by-guards -> pseudonymize, replay stays bit-identical; load-bearing data flagged); provably-safe redaction.
